content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA consisting of NNN integers.

Consider an array SSS of length NNN, such that each SiS_iSi​ is either +1+1+1 or −1-1−1.

For such an array, we define f(S)f(S)f(S) as follows:


Consider a 000-indexed array BBB of length N+1N+1N+1, where B0=0B_0 = 0B0​=0 and for 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N,

Bi=∑j=1iSj⋅AjB_i = \sum_{j=1}^i S_j\cdot A_jBi​=∑j=1i​Sj​⋅Aj​

Informally, BBB is the signed prefix sum array of AAA, with SSS giving the signs.
Define f(S):=max⁡(B)−min⁡(B)f(S) := \max(B) - \min(B)f(S):=max(B)−min(B).
For example, if A=[1,3,4,1]A = [1, 3, 4, 1]A=[1,3,4,1] and S=[1,−1,−1,1]S = [1, -1, -1, 1]S=[1,−1,−1,1] we obtain B=[0,1,−2,−6,−5]B = [0, 1, -2, -6, -5]B=[0,1,−2,−6,−5], and so

f(S)=max⁡(B)−min⁡(B)=1−(−6)=7f(S) = \max(B) - \min(B) = 1 - (-6) = 7f(S)=max(B)−min(B)=1−(−6)=7.

There are 2N2^N2N possible sign arrays SSS.

Given AAA, find the **minimum** possible value of f(S)f(S)f(S) across all these 2N2^N2N sign arrays, and also the number of sign arrays SSS that attain this minimum.

The number of sign arrays attaining the minimum can be large, so print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of elements in the array.
The second line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line two space-separated integers: the minimum possible value of f(S)f(S)f(S), and the number of arrays SSS that attain this minimum.

The second integer should be printed modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.
